request.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?

title: Jan Troell
Jason Bateman
A Frozen Dream
Hurricane (1979 film)
The Last Sentence
Bang! (film)
Land of Dreams (1988 film)
The New Land
Everlasting Moments
44th Guldbagge Awards
sentences: Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.
Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography.
His realistic films, with a lyrical photography in which nature is prominent, have placed him in the first rank of modern Swedish film directors along with Ingmar Bergman and Bo Widerberg.
Jason Kent Bateman (born January 14, 1969) is an American actor, director, and producer.
He began acting on television in the early 1980s on "Little House on the Prairie", and in the sitcoms "Silver Spoons" and "The Hogan Family".
In the 2000s, he became known for his role of Michael Bluth using deadpan comedy in the critically acclaimed sitcom "Arrested Development", for which he won a Golden Globe and a Satellite Award.

He has had starring roles in the films "Juno" (2007), "Hancock" (2008), "Up in the Air" (2009), "The Switch" (2010), "Paul" (2011), "Horrible Bosses" (2011), "The Change-Up" (2011), "Identity Thief" (2013), "Bad Words" (2013), "Horrible Bosses 2" (2014), "The Gift" (2015), and "Zootopia" (2016), as well as the 2017 Netflix series "Ozark".
A Frozen Dream ("En frusen dröm") is a documentary film released in 1997 as a followup to the 1982 Swedish movie "Flight of the Eagle", both directed by Jan Troell.
The documentary describes the ill-fated attempt to fly over the North Pole in a hydrogen balloon by Salomon August Andrée in 1897.
Hurricane is a 1979 romance and adventure film featuring Mia Farrow and Jason Robards, produced by Dino De Laurentiis and Lorenzo Semple, Jr., and directed by Jan Troell.
It is loosely based on the 1937 film of the same name.

The Last Sentence (Swedish: "Dom över död man; Judgement on the dead" ) is a Swedish film from 2012, directed by Jan Troell and starring Jesper Christensen, Pernilla August, Björn Granath and Ulla Skoog.

It is set between 1933 and 1945, and focuses on the life and career of Torgny Segerstedt, a Swedish newspaper editor who was a prominent critic of Hitler and the Nazis during a period when the Swedish government and monarch were intent on maintaining Sweden's neutrality and avoiding tensions with Germany.
The film also deals with Segerstedt's relations with his wife, his mistress, and his mistress's husband (who was a close friend of Segerstedt).
Bang!
is a 1977 Swedish drama film directed by Jan Troell.
It was entered into the 1977 Cannes Film Festival.
Håkan Serner won the award for Best Actor at the 13th Guldbagge Awards.
Land of Dreams is a 1988 Swedish essay film by Jan Troell.
Its original Swedish title is Sagolandet, which means "The land of tales".

Through a series of reportages from contemporary Sweden, Troell uses the film to ponder on the country's transformation since his childhood, into a society he argues has become permeated by rationality at the expense of creativity.

Interweaved with the reportages are conversations with the American existential psychologist Rollo May, the politician Ingvar Carlsson soon before he became the prime minister of Sweden, and former prime minister Tage Erlander.
Filming took place from 1983 to 1986.
The New Land (Swedish: Nybyggarna ) is a 1972 Swedish film co-written and directed by Jan Troell and starring Max von Sydow, Liv Ullmann and Eddie Axberg.
The film is a sequel to Troell's 1971 film "The Emigrants", and both are based on "The Emigrants" novels by Vilhelm Moberg.
Drawing its story from the last two novels, "The Settlers" and "The Last Letter Home", the film is about the Swedish immigrants establishing their home in Minnesota, during the Dakota War of 1862.
Everlasting Moments (Swedish: Maria Larssons eviga ögonblick ) is a 2008 Swedish drama film directed by Jan Troell, starring Maria Heiskanen, Mikael Persbrandt and Jesper Christensen.
It is based on the true story of Maria Larsson, a Swedish working class woman in the early 20th century, who wins a camera in a lottery and goes on to become a photographer.
It has been compared to Troell's previous films "Here's Your Life" and "As White as in Snow", which are both set around the same period.
The 44th Guldbagge Awards ceremony, presented by the Swedish Film Institute, honored the best Swedish films of 2008, and took place on 12 January 2009. "
Everlasting Moments" directed by Jan Troell was presented with the award for Best Film.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Jan Troell (Jan Gustaf Troell) and Jason Bateman (Jason Kent Bateman)

Facts supported by passages:
- Jan Troell: "Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931)"; identified as a "Swedish film director"; "Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography." Directed "Hurricane" (1979) (film directed by Jan Troell). Directed "Everlasting Moments" (2008). Made "Land of Dreams" (1988), original Swedish title "Sagolandet"; filming took place "from 1983 to 1986."
- Jason Bateman: "Jason Kent Bateman (born January 14, 1969)"; identified as an "American actor, director, and producer." Began acting on television "in the early 1980s" on "Little House on the Prairie", and sitcoms "Silver Spoons" and "The Hogan Family." Known for role Michael Bluth in "Arrested Development" (won a Golden Globe and a Satellite Award). Has starring film roles in many films and the 2017 Netflix series "Ozark."

Ambiguities / missing facts blocking a direct comparison:
- "More diverse career" is undefined (no metric supplied). Missing: a comparable list/count of distinct occupations/roles and types of credits for each person (e.g., director, screenwriter, cinematographer, producer, actor, voice actor, television vs film directing/producing).
- Candidate name variants: "Jan Troell" vs "Jan Gustaf Troell" (same person); "Jason Bateman" vs "Jason Kent Bateman" (same person).
- Missing bridge facts to compare diversity: full filmography/credit list for Troell showing roles per film (director, writer, cinematographer) and Bateman's directing/producing credits beyond acting.

Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
1. "Jan Troell" — fetch Wikipedia page; inspect lede/infobox, "Career", and "Filmography" sections for complete list of occupations and credit roles (director/writer/cinematographer) and genres.
2. "Jason Bateman" — fetch Wikipedia page; inspect lede/infobox, "Career", and "Filmography" sections for full list of occupations, directing/producing credits, voice work, and TV vs film roles.
3. "Jan Troell filmography" (or the "Filmography" section of Troell's page) — extract role-by-role credits (who he served as cinematographer, writer, director, etc.).
4. Search keywords if needed: "Jan Gustaf Troell occupations cinematographer screenwriter photographer" and "Jason Kent Bateman directing credits producer credits voice acting" to capture any roles not listed on the main pages.
response.hop2_query: How do Jason Bateman and Jan Troell careers differ occupations roles
hop2_docs: Hurricane (1979 film) | Hurricane is a 1979 romance and adventure film featuring Mia Farrow and Jason Robards, produced by Dino De Laurentiis and Lorenzo Semple, Jr., and directed by Jan Troell.  It is loosely based on the 1937 film of the same name.

Jan Troell | Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.  Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography.  His realistic films, with a lyrical photography in which nature is prominent, have placed him in the first rank of modern Swedish film directors along with Ingmar Bergman and Bo Widerberg.

Breaking the Rules (film) | Breaking the Rules is a 1992 American drama film directed by Neal Israel, executive produced by Larry A. Thompson, starring Jason Bateman, C. Thomas Howell, Jonathan Silverman and Annie Potts.  Jason's father, Kent Bateman, has a role in the movie as well.

Hamsun (film) | Hamsun is a 1996 internationally co-produced drama film directed by Jan Troell, about the later life of the Norwegian author Knut Hamsun (Max von Sydow), who, together with his wife Marie Hamsun (Ghita Nørby), went from being a national hero to a traitor after supporting Nazi Germany during their occupation of Norway during World War II.

How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love? | How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love?  is an educational video released in 1986, and then again in 1992 in a classroom version.  It stars Jason Bateman, Justine Bateman, and Ted Danson.  It's based on a comic book of the same name by Sol Gordon, who also appears in clips in the video giving a lecture to students.  The video is intended to give teenagers straight clear answers about love and sex in a way they can relate to.  It includes interviews of students from University High School talking about their experiences with love and sex, as well as monologues and advice from Danson and the Batemans, and a music video.  The extremely 80s hair and clothing styles, and the bizarre editing and graphics make it often referenced as an accidental comedy.

Disconnect (film) | Disconnect is a 2012 American drama film directed by Henry Alex Rubin and stars an ensemble cast, which includes Jason Bateman, Hope Davis, Frank Grillo, Andrea Riseborough, Paula Patton, Michael Nyqvist, Alexander Skarsgård, Max Thieriot and fashion designer Marc Jacobs in his debut acting role.  The film explores how people experience the negative sides of modern communication technology by following three interconnecting stories.

Jessie Bateman | Jessie Eliza Bateman (2 August 1877 – 14 November 1940) was an English stage actress.  Bateman began her career as a child actress.  After early success on tour in Shakespearean roles, she built her career both in London and foreign tours.  She had her greatest success in the early years of the 20th century, and her career spanned over half a century.
hop2_titles: How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love?
response.summary_2: Neither is clearly more diverse — both have comparably varied careers. Jan Gustaf Troell usually writes his own scripts, serves as his own director of photography, and directs films. Jason Kent Bateman has worked as an actor, director, and producer.
response.answer: I can’t determine which filmmaker has had a more diverse career from the provided summaries: summary_1 is inconclusive about a direct comparison, while summary_2 states that neither is clearly more diverse and both have comparably varied careers.
